misconceptionSummary: Students miss sides when counting, especially when a diagram has unlabelled or implied sides.

visuals.ruleText: Perimeter = total distance around the outside of a shape
visuals.subText: Add every side length — do not skip any, even if sides are equal

steps.narration: Perimeter is the total length of the boundary of a shape — the distance you would walk if you went all the way around the outside. Every side must be included in the sum.

lines.text: Strategy: tick each side as you add it
Start at one vertex, work clockwise
Stop when you reach the starting vertex

steps.narration: Tick or mark each side as you add its length. Start at any vertex, travel clockwise around the boundary, and stop when you return to the start. A tick on every side confirms none were skipped.

lines.text: Sides: 4 cm, 7 cm, 3 cm, 5 cm, 6 cm, 2 cm

steps.narration: Add all six side lengths: four, seven, three, five, six, two. Total: twenty-seven centimetres.

visuals.label: Tick each side → add all lengths → perimeter is the total

steps.narration: Twenty-seven centimetres. Ticking each side as you go prevents double-counting or missing any side.

misconceptionStrip.text: Common mistake: counting the number of sides rather than adding the lengths, or missing a side on an irregular polygon. Mark each side as you include it in the sum.
misconceptionStrip.audioNarration: Tick every side as you add it. Work clockwise from one vertex back to the start — every side gets exactly one tick and one contribution to the total.
